title: Dell: Applying Its Business Value Model to Storage
author: David Hill; Peter Kastner
abstract: Aberdeen Group profile examining how Dell applies its direct business model and supply chain management expertise to the enterprise storage market. The report covers Dell's partnership with EMC for the CX disk array family (CX300/CX500/CX700), PowerVault NAS products, tape backup solutions, and storage management software. Key strategies include continuous price/performance improvement, driving industry standards, moving high-end functionality downstream to workgroups, software bundling to reduce complexity, and tighter product integration. The new CX generation delivers 25-100% performance improvements over predecessors while maintaining price parity.
importance_rationale: Documents Dell's strategic expansion from PCs/servers into enterprise storage through the EMC partnership; captures a pivotal moment in storage industry consolidation
relevance_rationale: Dell's storage strategy evolved significantly after the 2016 EMC acquisition; the standards-based and bundling approaches described remain relevant patterns
prescience_rationale: Correctly predicted standards-based storage would win over proprietary; the Dell-EMC partnership deepened into a full acquisition in 2016
